vocabulary.example: She had a very stern face and Harry’s first thought was that this was not someone to cross.
and a magnificent marble staircase facing them led to the upper floors.
No one was talking much except Hermione Granger, who was whispering very fast about all the spells she’d learned and wondering which one she’d need.

vocabulary.definition: to breathe in suddenly from shock
see-through; you can see through it
vocabulary.example: Pearly white and slightly transparent, they glided across the room talking to one another and hardly glancing at the first years.
Harry had never even imagined such a strange and splendid place.

vocabulary.definition: to become worn and ragged at the edges
vocabulary.example: This hat was patched and frayed and extremely dirty.

vocabulary.definition: to walk in a proud, show-off way
vocabulary.example: Malfoy swaggered forward when his name was called and got his wish at once: the hat had barely touched his head when it screamed, “SLYTHERIN!”

vocabulary.definition: to make someone feel less worried or afraid
vocabulary.example: He was so relieved to have been chosen and not put in Slytherin, he hardly noticed that he was getting the loudest cheer yet.
He was looking very peculiar in a large purple turban.
First years should note that the forest on the grounds is forbidden to all pupils.

vocabulary.definition: a mischievous spirit that causes trouble
vocabulary.example: “Peeves,” Percy whispered to the first years. “A poltergeist.”

characters.name: Harry Potter
characters.description: A nervous first year about to be Sorted in front of the whole school.

characters.name: Professor McGonagall
characters.description: The stern deputy headmistress who leads the first years and runs the Sorting.

characters.name: Ron Weasley
characters.description: Harry’s new friend, also waiting to be Sorted into a house.

characters.name: Nearly Headless Nick
characters.description: The Gryffindor ghost who introduces himself at the feast.

characters.name: Albus Dumbledore
characters.description: The headmaster who welcomes students and gives start-of-term notices.

background.hook: Harry arrives at Hogwarts for the Sorting Ceremony. A patched old hat will decide which of four houses he belongs to—while hundreds of students and teachers watch.
background.readingFocus: Notice how each student is Sorted, what the hat says to Harry, and the strange pain Harry feels when Snape looks at him during the feast.

miniCheck.stem: Someone with a stern face looks —
If something is forbidden, it is —

comprehension.stem: What is this chapter mainly about?

options.text: First years are Sorted into Hogwarts houses, enjoy the welcome feast, and reach Gryffindor Tower
Harry buys his school supplies in Diagon Alley
Harry learns to fly on a broomstick
Harry visits the Dursleys during summer holiday

comprehension.explanation: The chapter covers the Sorting Ceremony, the banquet, Dumbledore’s notices, and the first years finding their dormitory.

comprehension.stem: What are the four Hogwarts houses?
comprehension.explanation: Professor McGonagall names all four houses before the Sorting begins.

comprehension.stem: Which house does the Sorting Hat choose for Harry?
comprehension.explanation: After Harry thinks “Not Slytherin,” the hat shouts “GRYFFINDOR!” to the whole hall.

comprehension.stem: What are the four words in Dumbledore’s welcome speech?
comprehension.explanation: Dumbledore says those four words, thanks everyone, and sits back down.

comprehension.stem: How does Harry feel before the Sorting Ceremony?

options.text: Extremely nervous about a test in front of the whole school
Bored and eager to go to bed
Confident because he knows many spells

comprehension.explanation: Harry’s heart jolts at the idea of a test; he’d never been more nervous.

comprehension.stem: What happens when Professor Snape looks at Harry during the feast?

options.text: A sharp, hot pain shoots across Harry’s scar
Snape gives Harry a house point

comprehension.explanation: Snape looks past Quirrell’s turban into Harry’s eyes and Harry claps a hand to his head.

comprehension.stem: What password does Percy use to open the Gryffindor common room?
comprehension.explanation: Percy says “Caput Draconis” and the portrait swings open.

vocabulary.explanation: The ghosts are pearly white and slightly transparent as they glide through the room.

vocabulary.stem: Malfoy ___ forward when his name was called and got his wish at once.
vocabulary.explanation: Malfoy walks proudly to the stool and is Sorted into Slytherin immediately.

options.text: a mischievous spirit that causes trouble

vocabulary.explanation: Percy identifies Peeves as a poltergeist when walking sticks fly at him.

vocabulary.stem: Which word is closest in meaning to 'splendid'?
vocabulary.explanation: Harry finds the Great Hall a strange and splendid place.

vocabulary.stem: This hat was patched and ___ and extremely dirty.
vocabulary.explanation: The Sorting Hat looks old and worn before it sings to the hall.

cloze.title: The Sorting Hat
paragraphs: Professor McGonagall had a (1) ___ face as she led the first years into the castle. Harry (2) ___ when ghosts streamed through the wall, then entered the (3) ___ Great Hall.
A patched, (4) ___ hat sang and then Sorted each student. Malfoy (5) ___ forward into Slytherin, but Harry was (6) ___ to land in Gryffindor instead.
At the feast Dumbledore warned that the forest was (7) ___. On the way to bed, Peeves the poltergeist caused trouble before Percy gave the password.

gaps.explanation: McGonagall’s stern face makes Harry think she is not someone to cross.
Harry gasps when the ghosts appear behind him.
Harry had never imagined such a strange and splendid place.
The Sorting Hat is patched and frayed.
Malfoy swaggered forward confidently when called.
Harry is relieved not to be put in Slytherin.
Dumbledore says the forest is forbidden to all pupils.
